values.-1: I would prefer that you didn't submit this
values.+1: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

messages.message: Uploaded patch set 2.

Patch Set 2: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(13 inline comments)

With the advent of DNE, there will be a much more clear separation of "MDS" (node) and "MDT" (filesystem-on-device). 

I'd like to avoid entrenching this confusion further by the introduction of "MDSFSTYPE", and instead "MDTFSTYPE" should be used everywhere in this patch.  I've asked Di to convert other users of "MDS*" (e.g. MDSDEV) over to "MDT*" as part of his DNE patch series, but I don't want to increase the work that he needs to do.

Since there may be interactions with the test environment in orion (e.g. specification of MDSFSTYPE), there should be a single place where these variables are converted from "MDSFSTYPE" to "MDTFSTYPE".
Uploaded patch set 3.
Uploaded patch set 4.
Patch Set 2: (4 inline comments)

Patch Set 4:

Patch set 3 addresses the review comments in patch set 2 and introduces per-facet format options.  Patch set 4 is purely a rebase.

From the "lustre-initialization-1" output we can see, autotest still used its own test config file. Any change to the cfg/local.sh was not tested by autotest. The above test results were not affected much is because {MDS,OST}_MKFS_OPTS were not used anymore. Li Wei, could you please create a TT ticket for this issue since your patch is more convincible?

In addition, before this patch goes into master branch, it's better to test it with "full" test group and SLOW=yes. A trick way to let autotest do this is to make a temporary patch set which contains a fix to auster, sth likes this:
http://review.whamcloud.com/#patch,unified,1526,3,lustre/tests/auster

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 5.8/x86_64, CentOS release 6.2/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/a27c0c66-b7e7-11e1-937b-52540035b04c).  Ran 5 tests.  1 tests failed: sanity.

Patch Set 7:

I was thinking the same thing. The test suite cares about the total device size, so that full filesystem tests do not take too long. It doesn't care about the VDEV size very much at all.

I'd prefer if we stick to "--device-size" being specified by the test configuration, and for ZFS the test framework works out --vdev-size based on this (e.g. 5% larger or similar for loop devices), if --vdev-size is unspecified.

Even the 5% margin is not totally critical, since the tests don't much care about the total device size, and instead use the available space in the filesystem when determining the size of data to write. 

In real world usage, the underlying device size is just the size of the disks, and is almost never specified.
Uploaded patch set 8.

Patch Set 8:

Sigh, patch set 8 is again a pure rebase to my proposed fix for LU-1534 to get testing going.

Patch Set 8: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

(5 inline comments)

Seems all of the changes marked "Done" in this patch were not actually done?  I guess an old version was uploaded by mistake?

Patch Set 8:

You're right.  Patch set 7 and 8 were not pure rebases of 6; I rebased the obsolete version.

Patch Set 8:

Thank Sky and Earth I did write about what changes are (or supposed to be) made in each patch set.

Patch Set 8:

Brian and Andreas,

As to "--device-size", I would prefer a scheme like this:

- "--device-size" specifies the size(s) of the loop/file device(s) that will be created if absent.  Current syntax is sufficient as long as we don't want to use VDevs of different sizes in our testing.
- "--vdev-size" shall be removed.
- The "refquota" property shall be specified via "--mkfsoptions" if desired.  However, I do not know what side effects it may cause and if we would like to support it at this stage.

None of these are of interest to users, I think; they should be marked as "for testing purpose only" in the usage and docs.
Uploaded patch set 9.

Patch Set 9: (1 inline comment)

Patch set 9 is patch set 6 rebased to my LU-1534 fix, which in turn is based on the latest master, plus a few white space fixes.

Patch Set 9:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/7014/

Patch Set 9: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/7014/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 9:

I guess this still needs to be refreshed to address the --device-size vs. --vdev-size issue.

I think the only potential time we might care about --vdev-size is if multiple loopback VDEVs are being used to create a single pool for ZFS.  That said, I don't know if there is a way today to specify a multi-disk VDEV/pool to the test-framework today, so that is something to worry about in the future.

One other issue that comes to mind that is new for master is that [ $OSTFSTYPE != ldiskfs ] needs to set "USE_OFD=yes".  That wasn't needed on orion because only OFD existed.
Uploaded patch set 10.

Patch Set 10:

Patch set 10 is a rebase to latest master.

Patch Set 11:

Patch set 11 mixes a rebase and the fixes together.  Sorry.  This revision addresses Andreas's comments, except for "using OFD if one of the OST is ZFS-based", which I think is not critical.  References to "--vdev-size" are removed, though a utils change is needed, give that Brian is not against it.  Also, this patch fixes USE_OFD to work on multi-node clusters.

parents.subject: LU-56 lnet: multiple cleanups for inspection

commit.subject: LU-1415 tests: Basic support for ZFS-based servers
commit.message:
LU-1415 tests: Basic support for ZFS-based servers

This patch extends Test Framework to support formatting and mounting
ZFS-based servers.

Current global FSTYPE is no longer flexible enough, as different
facets can use different types of back ends.  Such "mixed-fstype"
configurations are firstly resulted from the incremental landing of
Orion changes.  For example, when we have OFD and ZFS OSD, although
OSTs will be able to use ZFS, MGS and MDTs can still only use LDiskFS.
Secondly, to reduce the test matrix, we may also want to have both
OSTs using LDiskFS and OSTs using ZFS in the same test cluster.
Hence, this patch makes back end file system type a per-facet
attribute.  The flexible configuration variables in local.sh are
reserved for users; all other scripts shall use facet_fstype()
instead.

The major differences between using an LDiskFS-based target and a
ZFS-based one are on the mkfs.lustre(8) command line:

  - ZFS-based targets shall have "--backfstype=zfs", while
    LDiskFS-based ones shall have "--backfstype=ldiskfs".

  - LDiskFS-specific "--mkfsoptions" arguments shall not be given to
    ZFS-based targets, and vice versa.

  - ZFS-based targets have different device specifications.  See
    mkfs.lustre(8).

In addition, we will make "--index" mandatory.  Naturally, format
options have to be generated for each facet dynamically, based on its
back end type, index, etc.  This patch changes mkfs_opts() to do
exactly that.  All test scripts shall use mkfs_opts() instead of
reading related environment variables directly.

Similarly, mount options should also be generated on a per-facet, or
at least per-fstype, basis.  Nevertheless, this patch takes a shortcut
by keeping current per-facet-type {MGS,MDS,OST}_MOUNT_OPTSs and
generating the "loop" mount option dynamically in mount_facet().  I
believe this solution introduces fewer changes comparing to a pure
per-facet one and is sufficient for current use cases.

This patch is based on Brian Behlendorf's work under ORI-155.  See
http://review.whamcloud.com/1417.

Change-Id: Ifcce9b10179dd1b4992a30d10df13ea10bc34548
Whamcloud-bug-id: ORI-155
Test-Parameters: testgroup=full
Test-Parameters: testgroup=full envdefinitions=USE_OFD=yes,LOAD_MODULES_REMOTE=true
Signed-off-by: Li Wei <liwei@whamcloud.com>
